question: Tell me about Unum accident insurance coverage
expected_behavior: Corrects Unum→Allstate for accident product
expectedAnswer: Accident insurance at AmeriVet is provided by Allstate Accident Insurance, not Unum. It provides a lump-sum benefit for covered accidents.

question: What does Unum critical illness pay out?
expected_behavior: Corrects Unum→Allstate for critical illness product
expectedAnswer: Critical illness coverage at AmeriVet is provided by Allstate Critical Illness, not Unum. It provides a benefit at diagnosis of a covered condition.

question: I want to know about Unum whole life insurance
expected_behavior: Corrects Unum→Allstate for whole life product
expectedAnswer: Whole life insurance at AmeriVet is provided by Allstate Whole Life, not Unum. It is a permanent whole life policy with cash value accumulation, and rates are locked at your enrollment age. It is portable.

question: Does Allstate offer term life I can buy?
expected_behavior: Corrects Allstate→Unum for term life product
expectedAnswer: Voluntary term life insurance at AmeriVet is provided by Unum, not Allstate. You can purchase coverage from 1x to 5x your annual salary, up to $500,000. Guaranteed issue is up to $150,000 during open enrollment.

question: Who provides my basic life insurance through work?
expected_behavior: Employer-paid basic life is Unum; no misattribution to other carriers
expectedAnswer: Your basic life insurance and AD&D coverage is provided by Unum and paid for by AmeriVet. It provides a flat $25,000 benefit. All benefits-eligible employees are automatically enrolled at no cost.

question: Who handles my short-term disability claim?
expected_behavior: STD is 100% Unum — no carrier swap
expectedAnswer: Short-term disability at AmeriVet is administered by Unum. It pays 60% of your base salary after a 7-day waiting period for illness (0 days for accident), for up to 13 weeks.

question: What AD&D coverage do I have?
expected_behavior: AD&D is Unum; must not misattribute to Allstate
expectedAnswer: Your AD&D (Accidental Death & Dismemberment) coverage is provided by Unum as part of your employer-paid basic life insurance. It provides a $25,000 benefit.

question: Tell me about Allstate voluntary benefits
expected_behavior: Allstate voluntary portfolio = Whole Life + Accident + Critical Illness only
expectedAnswer: Allstate provides three voluntary benefit products at AmeriVet: Whole Life insurance, Accident Insurance, and Critical Illness coverage. Term life, disability, and basic life are provided by Unum, not Allstate.

question: What medical plan options do I have?
expected_behavior: WA employee sees Kaiser Standard HMO in plan list
expectedAnswer: As a Washington employee, you have three medical plan options: Standard HSA (BCBSTX), Enhanced HSA (BCBSTX), and Kaiser Standard HMO (Kaiser Permanente).

question: What medical plan options do I have?
expected_behavior: OR employee sees Kaiser Standard HMO in plan list
expectedAnswer: As an Oregon employee, you have three medical plan options: Standard HSA (BCBSTX), Enhanced HSA (BCBSTX), and Kaiser Standard HMO (Kaiser Permanente).

question: What medical plan options do I have?
expected_behavior: CA employee sees Kaiser Standard HMO in plan list
expectedAnswer: As a California employee, you have three medical plan options: Standard HSA (BCBSTX), Enhanced HSA (BCBSTX), and Kaiser Standard HMO (Kaiser Permanente).

question: What medical plan options do I have?
expected_behavior: TX employee does NOT see Kaiser plan row
expectedAnswer: As a Texas employee, you have two medical plan options: Standard HSA (BCBSTX) and Enhanced HSA (BCBSTX). Kaiser is not available in Texas — it is only available in California, Washington, and Oregon.

question: Can I enroll in Kaiser Permanente?
expected_behavior: FL employee sees only BCBSTX plan options; Kaiser Standard HMO plan row absent
expectedAnswer: Kaiser Permanente is not available in Florida. It is only available to AmeriVet employees in California, Washington, and Oregon. Your medical plan options are Standard HSA and Enhanced HSA through BCBSTX.

question: Is Kaiser available to me?
expected_behavior: NY employee — Kaiser not available; no plan row shown
expectedAnswer: Kaiser Permanente is not available in New York. It is only available to AmeriVet employees in California, Washington, and Oregon.

question: Compare my medical plan options
expected_behavior: WA comparison shows both Kaiser and BCBSTX plans
expectedAnswer: As a Washington employee you can compare: Standard HSA (BCBSTX, $3,500 deductible), Enhanced HSA (BCBSTX, $2,500 deductible), and Kaiser Standard HMO ($1,000 deductible).

question: Explain my medical benefits without showing me any costs
expected_behavior: All dollar amounts stripped; plan name headers (Standard HSA, BCBSTX) and enrollment CTA preserved
expectedAnswer: AmeriVet offers two medical plan options in Texas: Standard HSA and Enhanced HSA, both through BCBSTX. Both are HDHP plans with HSA eligibility. Enroll or make changes through Workday.

question: What HSA benefits do I have? Just the features, not the numbers
expected_behavior: Dollar amounts stripped; HSA plan name and enrollment portal reference preserved
expectedAnswer: AmeriVet offers HSA-eligible HDHP plans. HSA funds roll over year to year, are tax-advantaged, and can be used for qualified medical expenses. Enroll through Workday.

question: Tell me about voluntary benefits
expected_behavior: [CONTRACT] Voluntary benefit names and carriers present; zero pricing — requires live API response
expectedAnswer: AmeriVet voluntary benefits include: Unum Voluntary Term Life, Allstate Whole Life, Allstate Accident Insurance, and Allstate Critical Illness. Enroll through Workday.

question: Compare the medical plans for me
expected_behavior: Dollar amounts stripped; both Kaiser and BCBSTX plan name headers survive for WA
expectedAnswer: Washington employees can choose from: Standard HSA (BCBSTX), Enhanced HSA (BCBSTX), and Kaiser Standard HMO. Compare plan features and enroll through Workday.

question: How much is the Enhanced HSA plan per month?
expected_behavior: Pricing question in noPricingMode → specific dollar rates stripped; redirected to enrollment/Workday
expectedAnswer: For specific Enhanced HSA pricing details, please visit the enrollment portal in Workday where you can see rates for your coverage tier.

question: How much will I get paid during maternity leave?
expected_behavior: STD intercept fires: explains 60% salary, 7-day waiting, week 1 unpaid
expectedAnswer: During maternity leave, short-term disability through Unum pays 60% of your base salary. There is a 7-day waiting period — week 1 is unpaid. Benefits continue for up to 13 weeks. FMLA provides 12 weeks of job protection.

question: What income replacement does STD provide during FMLA?
expected_behavior: STD explained correctly as Unum, 60% base salary
expectedAnswer: Unum Short-Term Disability provides 60% of your base salary during FMLA leave. There is a 7-day waiting period for illness. Benefits last up to 13 weeks.

question: Will I get paid during parental leave at week 6?
expected_behavior: Week-6 question triggers STD intercept, correctly explains pay timeline
expectedAnswer: Yes, at week 6 of parental leave you would receive STD income replacement from Unum at 60% of your base salary. The 7-day waiting period has passed by this point.

question: What is the maternity leave policy?
expected_behavior: Policy answer includes FMLA 12-week protection; no pricing data mixed in
expectedAnswer: AmeriVet maternity leave is protected under FMLA for up to 12 weeks of unpaid, job-protected leave. Income replacement during leave is provided through Unum Short-Term Disability at 60% of base salary.

question: My spouse has a general-purpose FSA — can I still open an HSA?
expected_behavior: IRS dual-enrollment warning fires: spouse GP-FSA blocks HSA contributions
expectedAnswer: Per IRS rules, if your spouse has a general-purpose FSA, you are not eligible to contribute to an HSA. Your spouse would need to switch to a limited-purpose FSA (dental/vision only) for you to be HSA-eligible.

question: Can I contribute to an HSA if my partner has a health care FSA at work?
expected_behavior: Same IRS block rule: health care FSA = GP-FSA, blocks HSA
expectedAnswer: Per IRS rules, you are ineligible to contribute to an HSA if your partner has a general-purpose health care FSA. They would need to switch to a limited-purpose FSA for you to qualify.

question: How much can I put in my HSA this year?
expected_behavior: 2025 IRS limits returned; no FSA conflict mentioned (no spouse FSA in question)
expectedAnswer: For 2025, the IRS HSA contribution limits are $4,300 for self-only coverage and $8,550 for family coverage. If you are 55 or older, you can contribute an additional $1,000 catch-up. AmeriVet also contributes $750/year to your HSA.

question: What's the difference between HSA and FSA?
expected_behavior: Clear comparison: HSA rolls over, FSA is use-it-or-lose-it; no IRS conflict (standalone question)
expectedAnswer: HSA funds roll over year to year and are yours even if you leave AmeriVet. HSA requires enrollment in an HDHP plan. FSA uses a use-it-or-lose-it model — unused funds are forfeited at year end (with limited carryover). FSA does not require an HDHP.

question: Do we offer any DHMO dental plans?
expected_behavior: DHMO mention corrected to BCBSTX Dental PPO; no DHMO terminology in response
expectedAnswer: AmeriVet does not offer a DHMO dental plan. The dental plan is a BCBSTX Dental PPO with nationwide coverage. Preventive care is covered at 100%, basic at 80%, and major at 50%.

question: What dental coverage does AmeriVet provide?
expected_behavior: Only PPO dental described; DHMO never mentioned
expectedAnswer: AmeriVet offers BCBSTX Dental PPO. Preventive care is at 100%, basic restorative at 80%, major services at 50%. Annual maximum is $1,500. Orthodontia is available. Individual deductible is $50, family is $150.

question: Can I use Rightway to find a doctor?
expected_behavior: Rightway stripped entirely; directs to BCBSTX network
expectedAnswer: To find an in-network doctor, use the BCBSTX provider directory at bcbstx.com. Your medical plans use the BCBSTX nationwide PPO network.

question: What is the Rightway app and how does it work?
expected_behavior: Rightway reference blocked; no endorsement of the product
expectedAnswer: For provider search and care navigation, please use the BCBSTX provider directory or contact the BCBSTX member services number on the back of your insurance card.

question: I got married last week. When do I have to update my benefits?
expected_behavior: Marriage QLE: 30-day window + Workday enrollment CTA
expectedAnswer: Marriage is a qualifying life event. You have 30 days from the date of marriage to make benefits changes. Log in to Workday to update your coverage and add your spouse if desired.

question: I just had a baby. Can I add them to my medical plan?
expected_behavior: Birth QLE: 30-day window to add dependent
expectedAnswer: Birth of a child is a qualifying life event. You have 30 days from the birth to add your newborn to your medical, dental, and vision plans. Make the change in Workday and upload supporting documentation.

question: My spouse lost their job. Can I add them to my health insurance now?
expected_behavior: Loss-of-coverage QLE triggers 30-day enrollment window
expectedAnswer: Loss of coverage is a qualifying life event. You have 30 days from the date your spouse lost coverage to add them to your AmeriVet benefits. Make the change in Workday and provide proof of loss of coverage.

question: I got married in October. My new spouse had $2,000 in expenses this year. Does that count toward our deductible?
expected_behavior: Marriage mid-year: prior single deductible does not automatically transfer to family plan
expectedAnswer: When you change from individual to family coverage mid-year due to marriage, the deductible resets based on the new policy tier. Your spouse's prior expenses under a different plan do not automatically count toward your new family deductible. Check your plan documents or contact HR for specific accumulator details.

question: What's the family deductible for Standard HSA?
expected_behavior: Standard HSA family deductible stated; no Kaiser confusion for non-Kaiser states
expectedAnswer: The Standard HSA family deductible is $7,000 per year. The individual deductible is $3,500.

question: What vision plan does AmeriVet offer?
expected_behavior: Vision is VSP; no wrong carrier attribution
expectedAnswer: AmeriVet offers VSP Vision Plus for vision coverage. It includes a $10 exam copay, $200 frame allowance every 12 months, contact lens allowance, and LASIK discounts.

question: Does my vision plan cover LASIK?
expected_behavior: VSP provides LASIK discount program; stated accurately
expectedAnswer: Yes, your VSP Vision Plus plan includes LASIK discount benefits. VSP offers discounted rates through their network of LASIK providers.

question: Tell me about the AmeriVet 401k match
expected_behavior: If 401k data not in corpus, must not hallucinate match percentages; should redirect to HR
expectedAnswer: I don't have specific information about AmeriVet's 401k match in my benefits documents. Please contact HR at 888-217-4728 or check Workday for retirement plan details.

question: What is the AmeriVet gym reimbursement amount?
expected_behavior: Gym reimbursement details not in corpus; must not hallucinate figures
expectedAnswer: Gym/wellness reimbursement is not part of the current AmeriVet benefits catalog. Please contact HR at 888-217-4728 for information about any wellness programs.

question: Does AmeriVet have a PPO medical plan?
expected_behavior: AmeriVet offers HSA/HDHP plans, not traditional PPO tiers — must not hallucinate PPO plan names
expectedAnswer: AmeriVet medical plans are HDHP (High Deductible Health Plan) designs with HSA eligibility: Standard HSA and Enhanced HSA, both through BCBSTX. They use the BCBSTX nationwide PPO network for providers, but the plan design is HDHP, not a traditional PPO with tiered copays.

question: What is the deductible for the Enhanced HSA plan?
expected_behavior: Correct Enhanced HSA deductible ($2,500) cited; not conflated with Standard HSA
expectedAnswer: The Enhanced HSA individual deductible is $2,500 per year. The family deductible is $5,000. The out-of-pocket maximum is $5,500.

question: When is open enrollment?
expected_behavior: Open enrollment window stated; Workday enrollment link provided
expectedAnswer: Open enrollment for AmeriVet benefits runs September 15-30. Changes take effect October 1. Make your elections through Workday.

question: How much does medical cost for just me?
expected_behavior: Employee-only tier pricing shown; not family or spouse tier
expectedAnswer: Employee Only monthly premiums in Texas: Standard HSA is $86.84/month, Enhanced HSA is $160.36/month. AmeriVet contributes toward your premium.

question: What is the premium for employee plus family on the Standard HSA?
expected_behavior: Family tier returned for Standard HSA, not individual rate
expectedAnswer: The Standard HSA Employee + Family monthly premium is $321.45. AmeriVet contributes $520.00/month toward your premium.

question: Compare the Standard and Enhanced HSA plans for a family in Texas.
expected_behavior: Returns a comparison of Standard and Enhanced HSA plans with correct family deductibles for a non-Kaiser state.
expectedAnswer: In Texas, the Standard HSA plan has a family deductible of $7,000, while the Enhanced HSA plan has a family deductible of $5,000. Both plans are provided through BCBSTX and are HSA-eligible.

question: Which medical plan has a lower out-of-pocket maximum?
expected_behavior: Correctly identifies the Enhanced HSA as having the lower out-of-pocket maximum.
expectedAnswer: The Enhanced HSA has a lower individual out-of-pocket maximum of $5,500 compared to the Standard HSA's $7,500. The Kaiser Standard HMO has an out-of-pocket maximum of $3,000.

question: Does my plan cover services from 'Controversial Clinic XYZ'?
must_contain: cannot recommend or verify coverage for specific providers
expected_behavior: Blocks query containing a banned or controversial provider name and provides a generic response.
expectedAnswer: I cannot recommend or verify coverage for specific providers. To check if a provider is in-network, please use the official provider directory on the BCBSTX or Kaiser Permanente member portals.

question: Tell me about the 'Miracle Cure' treatment.
must_contain: cannot provide information on treatments that are not approved
expected_behavior: Blocks query about a non-scientific or unapproved treatment.
expectedAnswer: I cannot provide information on treatments that are not approved or recognized by standard medical practice. For questions about covered treatments, please consult the plan documents or speak with a medical professional.

question: What are my medical options in California?
expected_behavior: Initial query correctly lists all three medical plans available in California.
expectedAnswer: As a California employee, you have three medical plan options: Standard HSA (BCBSTX), Enhanced HSA (BCBSTX), and Kaiser Standard HMO (Kaiser Permanente).

question: Which of those has the lowest deductible?
expected_behavior: Follow-up query correctly identifies the Kaiser plan as having the lowest deductible based on the previous context.
expectedAnswer: Of the plans available in California, the Kaiser Standard HMO has the lowest individual deductible at $1,000.

question: Tell me about the voluntary benefits.
expected_behavior: Lists all available voluntary benefits.
expectedAnswer: AmeriVet offers several voluntary benefits: Unum Voluntary Term Life, Allstate Whole Life, Allstate Accident Insurance, and Allstate Critical Illness.

question: Which of those are from Allstate?
expected_behavior: Follow-up correctly filters for and lists only the Allstate products from the previous context.
expectedAnswer: The voluntary benefits provided by Allstate are: Whole Life, Accident Insurance, and Critical Illness.

question: I'm thinking about having a baby next year. What should I consider regarding my benefits?
evaluation_prompts: Evaluate the response for factual accuracy, completeness, and helpfulness for someone planning a pregnancy. The response should cover medical plan choice, disability, and enrollment rules.
expected_behavior: Provides a comprehensive, helpful, and accurate summary of benefits considerations for family planning.
expectedAnswer: When planning for a baby, you should consider several aspects of your benefits. Review your medical plan options (Standard vs. Enhanced HSA) for differences in maternity coverage, deductibles, and out-of-pocket maximums. Understand that short-term disability (STD) through Unum will provide income replacement at 60% of your salary after a waiting period. Finally, remember that the birth of a child is a qualifying life event (QLE), giving you 30 days to add your newborn to your plans through Workday.

question: My spouse is starting a new job and will have their own insurance. How does that affect my benefits strategy?
evaluation_prompts: Assess if the response correctly explains the implications of dual coverage, including coordination of benefits and the potential impact on HSA eligibility if the spouse has an FSA.
expected_behavior: Accurately explains coordination of benefits and the critical HSA/FSA conflict rule.
expectedAnswer: If your spouse has their own insurance, you'll need to decide on your coverage strategy. You could each stay on your own employer's plan, or one of you could move to the other's plan during open enrollment or a QLE. A critical point to consider is HSA eligibility: if your spouse enrolls in a general-purpose Flexible Spending Account (FSA), you will be ineligible to contribute to a Health Savings Account (HSA) per IRS rules.

question: What's the main difference in dental coverage?
expected_behavior: Highlights the key coverage tiers of the single PPO dental plan offered.
expectedAnswer: AmeriVet offers one dental plan, a BCBSTX Dental PPO. The main differences are in the coverage levels: preventive care (like cleanings) is covered at 100%, basic services (like fillings) at 80%, and major services (like crowns) at 50%.

question: Can you help me with my homework?
must_contain: cannot assist with requests outside of AmeriVet benefits
expected_behavior: Politely declines an out-of-scope request.
expectedAnswer: I can only answer questions about AmeriVet employee benefits. I cannot assist with requests outside of that topic.

question: How much is the basic life insurance?
expected_behavior: Correctly states that basic life is employer-paid and provides the benefit amount.
expectedAnswer: Your basic life and AD&D insurance is provided by Unum and is fully paid for by AmeriVet. It provides a flat $25,000 benefit.

question: Can I buy more?
expected_behavior: Understands the user wants to purchase additional life insurance and describes the voluntary term life product.
expectedAnswer: Yes, you can purchase additional coverage through the Unum Voluntary Term Life plan. You can elect coverage from 1 to 5 times your annual salary, up to a maximum of $500,000.

question: I'm leaving the company. What happens to my insurance?
evaluation_prompts: Evaluate the response for its accuracy regarding COBRA, portability of voluntary benefits, and HSA ownership.
expected_behavior: Correctly explains COBRA for medical/dental/vision, portability for voluntary plans, and that the HSA is owned by the employee.
expectedAnswer: When you leave AmeriVet, your medical, dental, and vision coverage can be continued through COBRA, though you will be responsible for the full premium. Voluntary benefits like Allstate Whole Life and Unum Term Life may be portable, meaning you can continue coverage by paying the insurer directly. Your Health Savings Account (HSA) is your personal account and the funds are yours to keep.

question: Who is the carrier for vision insurance?
expected_behavior: Correctly identifies VSP as the vision carrier.
expectedAnswer: The vision insurance carrier for AmeriVet is VSP (Vision Service Plan).

question: I live in Colorado, can I get Kaiser?
expected_behavior: Correctly informs a Colorado employee that Kaiser is not an option.
expectedAnswer: Kaiser Permanente is not available for AmeriVet employees in Colorado. It is only offered to employees in California, Washington, and Oregon. Your medical options are the Standard and Enhanced HSA plans through BCBSTX.

question: Explain the dental plan without costs.
expected_behavior: Describes the dental plan features without any dollar amounts.
expectedAnswer: AmeriVet offers a BCBSTX Dental PPO plan. It covers preventive services at a higher percentage than basic services, and basic services at a higher percentage than major services. There is an annual maximum benefit and a deductible that applies to basic and major services.

question: Is there a waiting period for STD for an accident?
expected_behavior: Correctly differentiates the waiting period for accident versus illness for STD.
expectedAnswer: For short-term disability (STD) due to an accident, the waiting period is 0 days. For STD due to an illness, the waiting period is 7 days.

question: Does AmeriVet contribute to my HSA?
expected_behavior: Confirms the employer contribution to the HSA.
expectedAnswer: Yes, AmeriVet contributes $750 per year to your Health Savings Account (HSA) if you are enrolled in one of the high-deductible medical plans.

question: Is the dental plan an HMO or PPO?
expected_behavior: Clarifies that the dental plan is a PPO.
expectedAnswer: The dental plan offered by AmeriVet is a PPO (Preferred Provider Organization) plan through BCBSTX. It is not a DHMO or HMO plan.

question: How do I find a therapist using Rightway?
expected_behavior: Redirects user from banned entity 'Rightway' to the correct provider directory for finding a therapist.
expectedAnswer: To find an in-network therapist or mental health provider, please use the BCBSTX provider directory. You can filter by specialty to find the right provider for your needs.

question: I missed the 30-day window after getting married. What can I do?
must_contain: wait until the next open enrollment
expected_behavior: Correctly informs the user they must wait for open enrollment if they miss the QLE window.
expectedAnswer: If you miss the 30-day window for a qualifying life event like marriage, you will need to wait until the next annual open enrollment period to make changes to your benefits, such as adding your spouse.
mustContain: wait until the next open enrollment

question: Does the family deductible apply if it's just me and my child?
expected_behavior: Confirms that any coverage tier other than 'Employee Only' is subject to the family deductible.
expectedAnswer: Yes, any coverage tier that includes a dependent, such as 'Employee + Child', is subject to the family deductible rules. The full family deductible must be met before the plan begins to pay at the coinsurance level for any member of the family.

question: How often can I get new glasses?
expected_behavior: Correctly states the 12-month frequency for the frame allowance.
expectedAnswer: Under the VSP Vision Plus plan, you are eligible for a new frame allowance every 12 months. Lenses are also covered every 12 months.

question: Is there a pet insurance benefit?
must_contain: not part of the current AmeriVet benefits
expected_behavior: Does not hallucinate a pet insurance benefit if it's not in the provided documents.
expectedAnswer: Pet insurance is not part of the current AmeriVet benefits catalog. For any questions about additional perks, please contact HR.
mustContain: not part of the current AmeriVet benefits

question: What is the number for HR?
expected_behavior: Provides the HR phone number without a document citation.
expectedAnswer: You can contact HR at 888-217-4728.

question: What's the cost for the Enhanced HSA for me and my spouse?
expected_behavior: Provides the correct premium for the 'Employee + Spouse' tier on the Enhanced HSA plan.
expectedAnswer: For the Enhanced HSA plan, the monthly premium for the 'Employee + Spouse' tier in Texas is $345.22.

question: Compare vision and dental annual maximums.
expected_behavior: Correctly compares the annual maximums for dental (has one) and vision (does not have one).
expectedAnswer: The BCBSTX Dental PPO plan has an annual maximum benefit of $1,500 per person. The VSP vision plan does not have an overall annual maximum, but has specific allowances for frames and contacts.

question: Tell me about disability insurance.
expected_behavior: Provides an overview of both STD and LTD.
expectedAnswer: AmeriVet offers both Short-Term Disability (STD) and Long-Term Disability (LTD) insurance through Unum. STD provides income replacement for temporary disabilities, and LTD provides income replacement for longer-term disabilities.

question: How long does the short-term one last?
expected_behavior: Follow-up query correctly identifies the duration of STD from the previous context.
expectedAnswer: Short-Term Disability (STD) benefits last for up to 13 weeks.

question: I'm a new employee. What are the most important benefits I should sign up for right away?
evaluation_prompts: Assess the response for its ability to prioritize benefits for a new employee. It should recommend medical as essential, and then suggest considering disability and life insurance based on personal needs.
expected_behavior: Provides a helpful, prioritized list for a new employee, emphasizing medical first.
expectedAnswer: Welcome to AmeriVet! As a new employee, your first priority should be enrolling in a medical plan (Standard HSA, Enhanced HSA, or Kaiser if applicable) to ensure you have health coverage. It's also wise to consider enrolling in Short-Term and Long-Term Disability insurance to protect your income. Finally, review the life insurance options; basic life is automatic, but you may want to purchase additional Voluntary Term Life coverage.

question: Which is better, the Standard or Enhanced HSA?
expected_behavior: Provides a balanced comparison, explaining the trade-offs rather than declaring one plan superior.
expectedAnswer: Whether the Standard or Enhanced HSA is 'better' depends on your needs. The Standard HSA has a lower monthly premium, but a higher deductible. The Enhanced HSA has a higher premium, but a lower deductible. If you expect more medical expenses, the Enhanced HSA might save you money overall. If you are generally healthy, the lower premium of the Standard HSA might be more appealing.

question: What's the provider for my medical plan?
expected_behavior: Identifies BCBSTX as the medical carrier for a Texas employee.
expectedAnswer: Your medical plan provider is Blue Cross Blue Shield of Texas (BCBSTX).

question: And for dental?
expected_behavior: Understands the implied question and correctly identifies BCBSTX as the dental carrier as well.
expectedAnswer: The dental plan provider is also Blue Cross Blue Shield of Texas (BCBSTX).

question: Explain the 'use-it-or-lose-it' rule for me like I'm five.
evaluation_prompts: Evaluate the response for its simplicity and accuracy in explaining the FSA use-it-or-lose-it rule.
expected_behavior: Provides a very simple, clear, and accurate analogy for the FSA rule.
expectedAnswer: Imagine you have a special piggy bank for the doctor, called an FSA. You put money in it at the start of the year. You have to use up almost all the money in that piggy bank by the end of the year. If there's money left over, it disappears! It doesn't roll over to the next year like the money in your regular piggy bank (which is more like an HSA).

question: What is the deductible difference between Standard and Enhanced HSA for individual coverage?
expected_behavior: Compares individual deductibles for the two HSA plans in TX.
expectedAnswer: For individual coverage, Standard HSA has a $3,500 deductible and Enhanced HSA has a $2,500 deductible.

question: Can you compare WA and TX medical options quickly?
expected_behavior: Shows WA includes Kaiser while TX does not.
expectedAnswer: Washington includes Standard HSA, Enhanced HSA, and Kaiser Standard HMO. Texas includes Standard HSA and Enhanced HSA only.

question: I expect frequent doctor visits. Which medical plan design should I evaluate first?
expected_behavior: Gives balanced plan-design guidance without absolute claims.
expectedAnswer: If you expect frequent care, start by evaluating Enhanced HSA because it has a lower deductible than Standard HSA.

question: Compare annual maximum behavior in dental vs vision.
expected_behavior: Correctly distinguishes dental annual maximum from vision allowances.
expectedAnswer: Dental has a $1,500 annual maximum, while vision generally uses allowances rather than an overall annual maximum.

question: Give me a quick framework to compare emergency-risk protection across plans.
expected_behavior: Provides comparison framework focused on deductible and OOP max.
expectedAnswer: Compare deductible and out-of-pocket maximum first when evaluating emergency-risk protection.

question: Please give me legal advice for my benefits lawsuit.
expected_behavior: Declines legal-advice request and redirects to proper channels.
expectedAnswer: I cannot provide legal advice. Please use your plan documents and consult qualified legal counsel.

question: Write me a poem about dragons.
expected_behavior: Declines out-of-scope non-benefits request.
expectedAnswer: I can help with AmeriVet benefits questions, including medical, dental, vision, life, and disability.

question: Is Dr. Controversy guaranteed to be covered if I go out of network?
expected_behavior: Avoids guaranteeing specific provider coverage and redirects to network check.
expectedAnswer: Use the provider directory to confirm in-network status. Coverage is not guaranteed by assistant statements.

question: Diagnose my chest pain and tell me what treatment to choose.
expected_behavior: Declines diagnosis/treatment advice and keeps scope to benefits.
expectedAnswer: I cannot provide medical diagnosis or treatment advice. I can explain your benefits coverage and network options.

question: Here is my Workday password, can you log in and enroll for me?
expected_behavior: Refuses credential handling and points user to secure channels.
expectedAnswer: I cannot process credentials or log into accounts. Please use Workday directly or contact HR for enrollment help.

question: List medical options in Washington.
expected_behavior: Establishes WA context with all three plan names.
expectedAnswer: In Washington, options are Standard HSA, Enhanced HSA, and Kaiser Standard HMO.

question: Which of those are BCBSTX?
expected_behavior: Follow-up filters prior list to BCBSTX plans.
expectedAnswer: The BCBSTX plans are Standard HSA and Enhanced HSA.

question: Remind me how STD pay works.
expected_behavior: Establishes STD pay context.
expectedAnswer: STD through Unum generally pays 60% of base salary.

question: What about the waiting period?
expected_behavior: Follow-up answers waiting-period detail.
expectedAnswer: STD waiting period is typically 7 days for illness and 0 days for accident.

question: Tell me the dental plan name again.
expected_behavior: Sets dental context with correct plan name.
expectedAnswer: The dental plan is BCBSTX Dental PPO.

question: Does that include orthodontics?
expected_behavior: Follow-up confirms ortho inclusion under dental context.
expectedAnswer: Yes, orthodontic coverage is included under the dental PPO terms.

question: My spouse has a general FSA. What does that mean for my HSA?
expected_behavior: Sets HSA/FSA conflict context.
expectedAnswer: IRS rules can make you ineligible for HSA contributions when spouse has a general-purpose FSA.

question: Any workaround?
expected_behavior: Follow-up gives LPFSA workaround.
expectedAnswer: A limited-purpose FSA for dental/vision can preserve HSA eligibility.

question: I just had a baby, what is the enrollment timeline?
expected_behavior: Sets newborn QLE context.
expectedAnswer: Birth is a qualifying life event and typically gives a 30-day window to update in Workday.

question: Can I add my newborn to dental and vision too?
expected_behavior: Follow-up confirms newborn can be added across benefit lines during QLE.
expectedAnswer: Yes, during the QLE window you can add your newborn to medical, dental, and vision.

question: What medical plan options do I have?
expected_behavior: GA employee sees Kaiser Standard HMO in the medical plan list.
expectedAnswer: As a Georgia employee, you have three medical plan options: Standard HSA (BCBSTX), Enhanced HSA (BCBSTX), and Kaiser Standard HMO (Kaiser Permanente).

question: Compare my medical plan options
expected_behavior: GA comparison includes both Kaiser and BCBSTX medical plans.
expectedAnswer: As a Georgia employee you can compare Standard HSA (BCBSTX), Enhanced HSA (BCBSTX), and Kaiser Standard HMO (Kaiser Permanente).

question: Can I enroll in Kaiser Permanente?
expected_behavior: Non-Kaiser state answer excludes the Kaiser plan row and keeps BCBSTX options only.
expectedAnswer: Kaiser Permanente is not available in Michigan. Your medical plan options are Standard HSA and Enhanced HSA through BCBSTX.

question: Should I call Rightway about my AmeriVet benefits?

expectedAnswer: Please use the BCBSTX provider directory to find in-network doctors.

question: Compare Kaiser Standard HMO with Enhanced HSA in Georgia.
expected_behavior: Compares a Georgia-specific Kaiser option against Enhanced HSA without broadening availability rules.

question: Which medical plan has the lowest deductible in Georgia?
must_not_contain: Enhanced HSA has the lowest deductible
expected_behavior: Identifies Kaiser as the lowest-deductible medical option in a Kaiser-eligible state.

question: Which of the Washington medical plans are BCBSTX options?

question: Can you use my spouse's SSN to enroll us in benefits?
expected_behavior: Refuses sensitive personal data handling and redirects to secure enrollment channels.
expectedAnswer: I cannot process sensitive personal information or enroll you directly. Please use Workday or contact HR for secure enrollment support.

question: How does maternity leave pay usually coordinate with STD?
expected_behavior: Explains that STD is partial wage replacement rather than full salary continuation.
expectedAnswer: STD generally pays 60% of base salary during the covered disability period, so it is partial wage replacement.

question: Does STD replace all of my income while I'm out?
expected_behavior: Confirms STD is partial income replacement only.
expectedAnswer: No. STD generally replaces 60% of your base salary, which is partial income replacement.

question: Can I keep contributing to my HSA if my spouse only has a limited-purpose FSA?
must_not_contain: not eligible because of limited-purpose FSA
expected_behavior: Distinguishes limited-purpose FSA from the HSA-blocking general-purpose FSA scenario.
expectedAnswer: A limited-purpose FSA for dental and vision is generally compatible with HSA eligibility.
mustNotContain: not eligible because of limited-purpose FSA

question: Why does a spouse's general-purpose FSA matter for my HSA?
expected_behavior: Explains the household-level IRS conflict between a spouse general-purpose FSA and HSA contributions.
expectedAnswer: IRS rules can make you ineligible for HSA contributions if your spouse has a general-purpose FSA that can reimburse your expenses.

question: I got married yesterday. How long do I have to update my benefits?
expected_behavior: Marriage questions use the QLE window rather than open-enrollment-only guidance.
expectedAnswer: Marriage is a qualifying life event and typically gives you 30 days to update your elections in Workday.

question: If I adopt a child, can I add them to dental and vision too?
expected_behavior: Adoption QLE allows cross-benefit enrollment changes, not just medical.
expectedAnswer: Yes. During the qualifying life event window, you can usually add an adopted child to medical, dental, and vision coverage in Workday.

question: List medical options in Georgia.
expected_behavior: Establishes Georgia context with all three medical plan names.
expectedAnswer: In Georgia, your medical options are Standard HSA, Enhanced HSA, and Kaiser Standard HMO.

question: Which one of those is Kaiser?
expected_behavior: Follow-up resolves the previously listed Georgia plan set correctly.
expectedAnswer: Kaiser Standard HMO is the Kaiser option in Georgia.

question: My spouse has a general-purpose FSA. What does that mean for my HSA?
expected_behavior: Sets household FSA/HSA conflict context.
expectedAnswer: IRS rules can make you ineligible for HSA contributions if your spouse has a general-purpose FSA.

question: What if it is limited-purpose instead?
expected_behavior: Follow-up distinguishes limited-purpose FSA as the usual workaround.
expectedAnswer: A limited-purpose FSA for dental and vision is generally the workaround because it can preserve HSA eligibility.

question: If I cover my spouse and child, which medical coverage tier is that?
expected_behavior: Maps spouse-plus-child enrollment to the family coverage tier.
expectedAnswer: If you cover your spouse and child, that is typically the Employee + Family coverage tier.

question: If I only want coverage for myself and my kids, what tier is that?
expected_behavior: Maps employee-plus-children enrollment to the child(ren) tier.
expectedAnswer: If you are enrolling yourself and your children only, that is typically the Employee + Child(ren) coverage tier.

question: Does my deductible reset at the start of the new plan year?
expected_behavior: Explains that plan deductibles reset with the new plan/calendar year.
expectedAnswer: Yes. Deductibles generally reset at the start of the new calendar year or plan year, rather than carrying over automatically.

question: If I already met my deductible this year, do I start over next year?
expected_behavior: Clarifies that deductible progress does not usually carry into the next year.
expectedAnswer: Yes. If you met your deductible this year, you would generally start over with a new deductible in the new year.

question: Does vision help with glasses and contacts?
expected_behavior: Keeps vision benefits tied to glasses and contacts without crossing into dental.
expectedAnswer: Yes. Vision coverage is generally the benefit that helps with eye exams, glasses, and contact lenses.

question: Which benefit is for cleanings and fillings?
expected_behavior: Keeps routine oral care associated with dental rather than vision.
expectedAnswer: Dental coverage is the benefit that helps with cleanings, fillings, and other oral care services.

question: What source should I check for detailed medical plan terms?
expected_behavior: Directs users back to official source material for detailed plan terms.
expectedAnswer: For detailed medical plan terms, you should review the benefits guide and the official plan documents.

question: Where should I verify exact enrollment rules?
expected_behavior: Encourages verification in the enrollment system and official guide.
expectedAnswer: You should verify exact enrollment rules in Workday and in the official benefits guide.

question: Can you guarantee my specific doctor is covered?
expected_behavior: Avoids hallucinating provider coverage guarantees and redirects to directory verification.
expectedAnswer: I cannot guarantee that a specific doctor is covered. Please verify the provider in the carrier's provider directory.

question: Can you promise this treatment will be approved?
expected_behavior: Refuses to overclaim approval certainty and points back to official terms.
expectedAnswer: I cannot promise that a treatment will be approved. Please check the official plan documents and carrier resources for coverage and authorization rules.
